frameworks.name: competing stochastic integrations
frameworks.one_line: Two-level race-to-threshold among noise-driven meta-stable neural populations

frameworks.name: collective neural population dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Perceptual state emerges from collective interactions across populations

frameworks.name: distributed representation
frameworks.one_line: Sensory input and perceptual state encoded across multiple populations

frameworks.name: threshold decision mechanism
frameworks.one_line: Competition between alternative representations resolved by crossing a threshold

core_question: What collective neural mechanism generates the perpetual alternation observed in bistable perception?
key_assumptions: Individual neural populations are meta-stable, not bistable on their own
Sensory and perceptual content is distributed across multiple populations
Transitions between population states are noise-driven
Competition between alternatives is resolved by a threshold-crossing rule
manipulations: Varied number and interaction structure of meta-stable populations
Examined dependence on noise level and threshold parameters
key_findings: Collective dynamics produce bistable alternation independent of single-unit time-scales
Model reproduces wide range of observed mean alternation rates
Model reproduces consistent variability of successive dominance periods
Model reproduces stabilizing effect of past perceptual states
interpretations: Bistable perception reflects collective neural decision making rather than properties of individual populations
Model predicts previously unsuspected relationships between observable quantities of bistable perception
